generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance MotorShaftEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution encoder mounted directly on the motor shaft providing angular position data for real-time control feedback."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorShaftPositionData
]

instance GearboxOutputEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary encoder positioned behind the gearbox providing high-resolution angular position data for output shaft tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxOutputPositionData
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring input current to the permanent magnet synchronous motor for real-time operational monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentData
]

instance TorqueSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor capturing torque output from the motor-gearbox system for dynamic performance analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TorqueData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance MotorShaftPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorShaftEncoder
    base:desc "Time-series angular position data streamed via MQTT protocol from motor shaft encoder with near-real-time transmission."
]

instance GearboxOutputPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxOutputEncoder
    base:desc "Time-series angular position data streamed via MQTT protocol from gearbox output encoder with near-real-time transmission."
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
    base:desc "Time-series current data streamed via MQTT protocol from motor current sensor with real-time transmission."
]

instance TorqueData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TorqueSensor
    base:desc "Time-series torque data streamed via MQTT protocol from torque sensor with real-time transmission."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLRequirementsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) requirements model using SysML to represent system requirements across abstraction layers."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLToolchain
]

instance SysMLFunctionalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML functional architecture model representing system functions and their relationships using SysML block diagrams."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLToolchain
]

instance SysMLLogicalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML logical architecture model defining system components, interfaces, and interconnections using Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLToolchain
]

instance SysMLTechnicalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML product/technical layer model detailing physical components and their implementation using SysML parametric diagrams."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLToolchain
]

instance MontiArcComponentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Architecture Description Language (ADL) model using MontiArc to define system components, connectors, and composition."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcToolchain
]

instance MontiArcBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model using MontiArc Automaton to represent system state transitions and dynamic behavior."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcToolchain
]

instance SimulinkControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Detailed behavioral model implemented in MathWorks Simulink for real-time control system simulation and validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkToolchain
]

instance AggregatedSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated and abstracted sensor data traces collected from physical system for contextual analysis and monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorShaftPositionData, GearboxOutputPositionData, MotorCurrentData, TorqueData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationLayer
]

instance ProcessedControlData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed data from sensor streams after filtering, normalization, and feature extraction for real-time control processing."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorShaftPositionData, GearboxOutputPositionData, MotorCurrentData, TorqueData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlProcessingEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLToolchain : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MBSE toolchain implementing SysML methodologies for multi-layered system modeling across requirements, functional, logical, and technical layers."
    DTDFVocab:enables SysMLModelingService, SysMLValidationService
]

instance MontiArcToolchain : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ADL toolchain using MontiArc for component/connector architecture modeling and automaton-based behavioral descriptions."
    DTDFVocab:enables MontiArcArchitectureService, MontiArcBehaviorService
]

instance SimulinkToolchain : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation and analysis toolchain using MathWorks Simulink for detailed behavioral modeling and real-time control system validation."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulinkSimulationService, SimulinkControlService
]

instance DataAggregationLayer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware layer aggregating and abstracting heterogeneous sensor data streams into unified digital representations."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService, DataPreprocessingService
]

instance ControlProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time processing enabler interpreting sensor data and executing control commands for physical system actuation."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeControlService, FeedbackOptimizationService
]

instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Lightweight messaging protocol enabling real-time or near-real-time streaming of sensor time-series data to digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables SensorDataStreamingService
]

// C6: Services
instance SysMLModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing multi-layered system modeling using SysML methodologies for requirements, architecture, and technical specifications."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemRequirementsInsight, SystemArchitectureInsight
]

instance SysMLValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service validating SysML models through simulation-based testing and formal verification methods."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelValidationInsight
]

instance MontiArcArchitectureService : DTDFVocab:Service [

]

]

    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationValidationInsight
]

instance SimulinkControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing real-time control algorithms in Simulink for physical system actuation."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAlgorithmInsight
]

instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service aggregating heterogeneous sensor data into contextual traces for digital twin analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataAggregationInsight
]

instance DataPreprocessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service performing filtering, normalization, and feature extraction on raw sensor data for quality assurance."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataQualityInsight
]

instance SensorDataStreamingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling real-time MQTT-based streaming of sensor time-series data to digital twin infrastructure."

]

instance RealTimeControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service processing sensor data in real-time to generate control commands for physical system actuation."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlCommandAction
]

instance FeedbackOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service optimizing system performance through closed-loop feedback mechanisms using processed sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationAction
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor interfaced with gearbox mechanisms translating rotational motion for system actuation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeControlService
]

instance GearboxMechanism : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [

    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeControlService
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system leverages real-time data synchronization between the physical and digital components, as evidenced by the streaming of sensor data via **MQTT**, a lightweight publish-subscribe protocol optimized for IoT and real-time applications. While the document does not specify explicit synchronization frequencies or latency requirements, the use of MQTT implies a low-latency, event-driven data exchange mechanism suitable for near-real-time or real-time operational monitoring. The temporal granularity is implicitly tied to the sensor data resolution and the frequency at which sensor readings are streamed, though no specific time scales (e.g., millisecond, second, or minute-level updates) are defined in the provided context. The system’s reliance on live data during operation further suggests a dynamic, time-sensitive architecture where data freshness is critical for accurate digital representation and decision-making."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s orchestration and coordination are implicitly tied to Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) methodologies, which employ multi-layered abstraction (requirements, functional, logical, and product/technical layers) to manage complexity. Architecture description languages (ADLs) such as ACME, AADL, or MontiArc define component-connector perspectives, enabling integration of heterogeneous tools (e.g., SysML for structural modeling, Simulink for behavioral simulation, or C++/Java for code implementation). The system may adopt centralized or decentralized patterns, with coordination mechanisms derived from ADLs’ composition rules or external tools like simulation environments. However, the document does not specify explicit orchestration frameworks (e.g., microservices, event-driven architectures) or resource management strategies (e.g., load balancing, priority scheduling) for the Digital Twin constellation."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
  **Fidelity and Validity Considerations (Implied/Indirect):**

  1. **Model Fidelity Requirements:**
     - **Multi-Level Abstraction:** MBSE methodologies (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) require fidelity across layers (requirements to technical) to ensure consistency and accuracy.
     - **Behavioral Accuracy:** Integration with tools like Simulink or state machines implies high-fidelity behavioral modeling for dynamic systems.

  2. **Validation and Verification Methods:**
     - **Not explicitly detailed**, but implied through:
       - Use of standardized modeling languages (SysML, ADLs) for traceability and verification.
       - Combination with simulation tools (e.g., Simulink) for behavioral validation.

  3. **Uncertainty Sources and Quantification:**
     - **Not explicitly addressed**, but potential sources may include:
       - Data aggregation/abstraction in digital shadows (as per Kritzinger et al.).
       - Heterogeneous tool integration (e.g., discrepancies between SysML and Simulink models).

  4. **Error Detection and Correction Mechanisms:**
     - **Not specified**, but MBSE methodologies often include:
       - Model checking and consistency validation across layers.
       - Tool-specific mechanisms (e.g., Simulink’s simulation error handling).

  5. **Quality Assurance Processes:**
     - **Not explicitly described**, but implied by:
       - Use of structured methodologies (e.g., SMArDT) for systematic quality control.
       - Stakeholder integration to ensure alignment with requirements.
  "
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for the Digital Shadow system leverages the **MQTT protocol** for streaming sensor time-series data, ensuring lightweight and efficient communication between physical and digital layers. While the document does not explicitly detail the **network topology** (e.g., star, mesh, or hierarchical), the use of MQTT suggests support for **pub/sub architectures**, which are common in IoT and edge computing environments. Bandwidth requirements are implied to be **low-latency** to accommodate real-time data streaming, though specific latency thresholds or bandwidth capacities are not provided. The document does not mention **reliability mechanisms** (e.g., QoS levels in MQTT), **redundancy protocols**, or **security protocols** (e.g., TLS/SSL, authentication/authorization frameworks) for network communication."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates horizontally via **MQTT** for streaming time series data from sensors to the digital shadow, enabling real-time, data-driven interoperability with **IoT ecosystems** and other digital representations. The document does not specify additional protocols (e.g., OPC UA, REST, AMQP) or enterprise integration patterns, but the use of **MQTT** implies compatibility with lightweight, publish-subscribe architectures common in IoT and edge computing. Interoperability is further supported by the **MBSE toolchain**, which accommodates heterogeneous modeling notations (e.g., SysML, Simulink) and general-purpose languages (e.g., C++, Java), suggesting a **plug-and-play** approach for external systems. Dependencies on external systems are inferred to align with the **digital shadow’s passive data aggregation role**, where active processing (e.g., analytics, control logic) may reside in the Digital Twin layer. No explicit standards (e.g., OPC UA, FIWARE) or integration patterns (e.g., microservices, API gateways) are mentioned."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The provided context does not explicitly mention **industry standards**, **certification requirements**, or **compliance frameworks** for Digital Twin systems. However, the use of **SysML** (ISO/IEC 19505) and **ADLs** (e.g., AADL, ACME) implies adherence to **modeling standards** for system architecture and behavior. **Interoperability** is addressed through the integration of heterogeneous tools (e.g., Simulink, C++, Java) and modeling notations, suggesting compliance with **open standards** for data exchange and tool integration (e.g., **OMG standards** for SysML, **SAE AS5506** for AADL). The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Fig. 1) aligns with **manufacturing-specific standards** but does not specify broader certification or compliance frameworks."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates a **physical safety mechanism** via the **safety cell of the MABI Max-100-2-25-P robotic arm**, which restricts the maximum working range to ensure collision avoidance and operator safety during operation. No explicit cybersecurity protocols (e.g., authentication, access control, or threat mitigation strategies) are documented in the provided sources. The Asset Administration Shell (AAS) framework ([46], [47], [50]) enables structured data integration but does not specify security measures such as encryption, compliance with standards (e.g., IEC 62443, ISO 27001), or data protection mechanisms. The system leverages **MQTT (via Mosquitto [52])** for lightweight IoT communication, though no security extensions (e.g., TLS, OAuth) are mentioned. The **Functional Mockup Interface (FMI) [51]** supports simulation model exchange but lacks documented security considerations for data transmission or storage."
]
